meta.title: An Examination of Hardware Convergence in ICS
meta.description:
Todd Keller of INL

Begins with a comparison of biological convergence and cyber convergence, and why convergence is important in a security context.

It gets going at 5:35 when he talks about INL looked at convergence in smart meters. Not surprisingly, they found common chips in a variety of smart meters and how

Next they purchased a malware scanning kiosk in order to see if the vendor new the hardware and software component vulnerabilities that existed in their product. For example there was a Genesys Logic GL3220, which some say is vulnerable to BadUSB.

This is a good example of why a software bill of materials (SBOM) and hardware bill of materials are helpful. Ideally the vendors would monitor all software and hardware used for vulns, but this is not happening.

It's a short session, and the 8 minute Q&A at the end is a highlight.
